fault_hypothesis: The condition on line 71 incorrectly identifies cases where an element should be removed. This leads to `merged.pop()` on line 72 being called erroneously when both input lists end with the same value, causing the final list to be one element too short.

spec.expr: not (merged__1 and left and right and merged__1[-1] == left[-1] == right[-1])

constraints.intent: The special-case logic to remove a shared tail element is incorrect and should never be triggered.
When this branch is taken, it corrupts the list by removing an element, violating the length invariant.
When the buggy branch is not taken, the total number of items in the merged list is correct.
